data.narrative:
Hystar, a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $36M to date, is valued here against a frontier-tech peer set spanning 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Lanza Technologies, LNZA). The peer cohort in Climate & Energy at Series B stage includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), with a median valuation of $355M and range of $120M–$5.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, treating the $36M raised as a value proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yields $68M. Peer-median anchoring across the six same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $355M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in the climate tech space, where capital intensity, technology risk, and commercialization timelines vary substantially.

Triangulating these two approaches produces a valuation range of $58M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $212M. The material gap between stage-progression ($68M) and peer-median ($355M) suggests that Hystar's valuation depends critically on whether it demonstrates capabilities and market positioning closer to earlier-stage financial metrics or to the demonstrated progress of more mature peers. Key uncertainties include technology readiness level, capital requirements to commercialization, and competitive positioning relative to the named peer set.
